hypothesis: If generative models are pre-trained on large chemical databases (ChEMBL/ZINC), fine-tuned on the 103 curated CTF structures, and further optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-defined reward functions (flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric, and pattern-matching), then they will produce a higher percentage of valid, novel, internally diverse, and expert-filter-passing Covalent Triazine Framework candidates with lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance than models trained only on pre-training or without the expert reward-based transfer learning.
